audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim Decalogue retelling and Horeb mediation discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for present-generation covenant, Ten Words, Sabbath liberation rationale, parental honor, prohibitions, coveting, fire/voice revelation, people's fear, Moses' mediation, and command to walk without turning.

verses.restored: Moses called to all Israel and said to them: Hear, Israel, statutes and judgments I speak in your ears today; learn them and keep to do them.
YHWH our Divine cut a covenant with us at Horeb.
Not with our fathers alone did YHWH cut this covenant, but with us, we who are here today, all of us alive.
Face to face YHWH spoke with you at mountain from midst of fire.
I stood between YHWH and you at that time to declare to you word of YHWH, for you feared because of fire and did not go up mountain, saying:
I am YHWH your Divine, who brought you out from land of Egypt, from house of slaves.
You shall have no other gods before my face.
You shall not make for yourself carved image, any likeness of what is in heavens above or earth beneath or waters under earth.
You shall not bow to them or serve them, for I YHWH your Divine am a jealous Divine, visiting iniquity of fathers upon children to third and fourth of those hating me,
but doing covenant loyalty to thousands of those loving me and keeping my commands.
You shall not lift up name of YHWH your Divine for emptiness, for YHWH will not hold guiltless one who lifts his name for emptiness.
Keep Sabbath day to sanctify it, as YHWH your Divine commanded you.
Six days you shall serve and do all your work,

but seventh day is Sabbath to YHWH your Divine. You shall do no work—you, your son, your daughter, your male servant, your female servant, your ox, your donkey, any beast, or your sojourner within your gates—so your male servant and female servant may rest like you.
Remember you were a slave in land of Egypt, and YHWH your Divine brought you out from there with strong hand and outstretched arm; therefore YHWH your Divine commanded you to make Sabbath day.
Honor your father and your mother, as YHWH your Divine commanded you, so your days may lengthen and it may be well with you upon ground YHWH your Divine is giving you.
You shall not murder.
You shall not commit adultery.
You shall not steal.
You shall not answer against your neighbor as false witness.
You shall not covet your neighbor's wife, and you shall not desire your neighbor's house, field, male servant, female servant, ox, donkey, or anything belonging to your neighbor.

These words YHWH spoke to all your assembly at mountain from midst of fire, cloud, and thick gloom, with a great voice, and he added no more; he wrote them on two tablets of stone and gave them to me.
When you heard voice from midst of darkness while mountain burned with fire, you came near to me, all heads of your tribes and elders.
You said, Look, YHWH our Divine has shown us his glory and greatness, and his voice we heard from midst of fire. This day we saw that Divine speaks with human and he lives.
Now why should we die? For this great fire will consume us. If we continue to hear voice of YHWH our Divine anymore, we shall die.
For who of all flesh has heard voice of living Divine speaking from midst of fire as we have and lived?
You draw near and hear all YHWH our Divine says, and you shall speak to us all YHWH our Divine speaks to you; we will hear and do.
YHWH heard voice of your words when you spoke to me, and YHWH said to me, I have heard voice of words of this people that they spoke to you; they have done well in all they spoke.
Who would give that this heart of theirs were in them to fear/revere me and keep all my commands all days, so it might be well with them and their children forever?
Go say to them, Return to your tents.
But you stand here with me, and I will speak to you all command, statutes, and judgments you shall teach them, that they may do them in land I give them to possess.
You shall keep to do as YHWH your Divine commanded you; you shall not turn aside right or left.
In all way YHWH your Divine commanded you, you shall walk, so you may live and it may be well with you and you may lengthen days in land you shall possess.

translation_notes: Deuteronomy presents the Horeb covenant as directly present to the living generation, making memory participatory rather than merely ancestral.
The Sabbath rationale centers liberation: servants, animals, and sojourners rest because Israel remembers slavery.
Visiting ancestral iniquity language must not be used to blame children for abuse, illness, disability, or family trauma; the text contrasts limited consequence with expansive covenant loyalty.
Parental honor never requires submission to abuse or concealment of harm.
The coveting list reflects an ancient household economy in which wife and servants appear within possessions; this structure must not define modern personhood.
The people's request for mediation is affirmed, while Moses remains a teacher rather than replacement for direct covenant responsibility.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 5 to justify inherited guilt, abusive parental control, ownership of spouses or servants, religious coercion, or antisemitic claims that Torah is merely oppressive law. Center liberation, universal rest within the household, truth, non-murder, and covenant responsibility.
